page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory and compression capabilities. The server achieves strong results in large-block memory bandwidth, with read-only speeds of 210.08 GB/sec and write speeds of 98.02 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 31.99 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores are strong, registering at 49425.07 and 2940.23 respectively. However, multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall into the average tier, and LLM text generation speeds are weak to poor, with the medium 7B model generating only 7.8 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare-metal instance offers a high density of DDR4 memory and high-frequency processing cores, making it suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without the overhead of virtualization. Given the lack of local storage and GPUs, workloads must rely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound execution. The server is suited for large in-memory databases, memory-intensive analytics, enterprise applications, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded gzip compression, alongside strong Passmark CPU and memory scores. Conversely, LLM text generation performance is weak to poor, and database operations are average. This server is suited for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large in-memory databases, and high-throughput web serving where massive memory capacity and high CPU clock speeds are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal server with 48 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and in-memory databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal server with 48 vCPUs, 1.5 TB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring high-frequency processors and massive RAM capacity for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8252C at 4.5 GHz
1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Strong large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression benchmarks
Suited for in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive analytics
